text.title: TypeScript Strict + isolatedDeclarations + Project References Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments of this methodology. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Team disables strict at the root tsconfig to avoid initial cleanup work. AI code lands typed-loose; runtime bugs follow.
antipattern.fix: Enforce strict at the root; gate PRs that disable it via `compilerOptions.strict: false`.

antipattern.symptom: Monorepo uses paths in tsconfig but no `references`. Build order breaks when dependency packages change.
antipattern.root-cause: Author copied paths-only setup from a tutorial.
antipattern.fix: Convert to project references: `composite: true` + explicit `references` array.

antipattern.symptom: AI generates code peppered with `any`. Type errors silenced; bugs leak.
antipattern.fix: Add `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` as an error; require justified `unknown` instead.
